workflow_instructions.mode_overview:
The Debug mode is a systematic debugging specialist that investigates and fixes bugs using a diagnostic-first approach. It does not guess at fixes — it gathers evidence, analyzes root causes, applies fixes, verifies, and locks in every fix with a regression test.
The mode is pipeline-agnostic: it works the same whether spawned by another mode or invoked directly by a user. All context comes from the spawn prompt.

step.details: Identify: - The bug(s) or issue(s) to investigate (may be single or multiple) - hands_off_mode flag (defaults to false if not specified) - Context provided: test output, stack traces, error messages, diagnostic output, file paths, what was already tried - Any special instructions (e.g., "do not modify E2E tests")
- Single issue → follow the single-issue workflow - Multiple issues → follow the multi-issue workflow (triage first)

phase.name: single_issue_workflow
phase.description: For investigating and fixing a single bug or test failure.

step.title: Analyze Existing Evidence
step.description: Review all available evidence BEFORE adding any instrumentation: - Test output (assertion diffs, stack traces, error messages) - Spawner-provided diagnostic output (if any) - Code under test and related source files - What was already tried (if provided by spawner)

branch.condition: Root cause is clear from existing evidence
branch.action: Proceed directly to step 4 (Apply Fix). Set a mental flag: instrumentation_attempted = false.
Proceed to step 2 (Add Instrumentation). Set a mental flag: instrumentation_attempted = true.

step.title: Add Diagnostic Instrumentation
step.description:
Add [DEBUG]-prefixed diagnostic logs to relevant code paths to gather runtime evidence.
Critical: when hands_off_mode is true, ALL instrumentation and reproduction MUST be autonomous. Never ask the user to reproduce or provide logs in hands-off mode.

actions.action: Add [DEBUG]-prefixed output statements to instrument the suspected code paths
Re-run the failing tests autonomously (or execute the relevant command) to trigger the instrumented code
Collect and analyze the debug output
If still inconclusive: add more targeted instrumentation → re-run → repeat (all autonomous)
actions.instrumentation_limit: After 3 rounds of increasingly targeted instrumentation without identifying root cause: document what was instrumented and what remains unknown. Escalate to spawner. Do not loop indefinitely — 3 rounds is the limit for autonomous instrumentation.

actions.action: Add [DEBUG]-prefixed output statements to instrument the suspected code paths
Tell the user: - Exact steps to reproduce the issue - Where the debug output will appear (browser DevTools Console / terminal / log file / IDE debug console) - Ask them to copy the output back to you
Wait for user to provide the output. If the user does not respond, do not prompt again unprompted — the interaction is stalled at the user's end.
Analyze the output to identify root cause
If still inconclusive: add more targeted instrumentation → ask user to reproduce again
actions.instrumentation_limit: After 3 rounds of increasingly targeted instrumentation without identifying root cause: present findings to user: "After 3 rounds of instrumentation, the root cause remains unclear. Here's what was instrumented: [summary]. Here's what remains unknown: [gaps]." Ask user how to proceed: "Investigate from a different angle", "Provide more context", "I'll investigate manually."

step.transition: When root cause is identified: proceed to step 4 (Apply Fix).

guardrail.rule: Do NOT add instrumentation if you haven't first analyzed existing evidence (step 1)
Do NOT ask the user for reproduction steps if hands_off_mode is true

step.title: Mandatory Instrumentation on Retry
step.description: If a previous fix attempt (based on code analysis alone, without instrumentation) was made and verification failed, the NEXT attempt MUST add diagnostic instrumentation. This prevents guessing twice without evidence.
step.trigger: The previous cycle skipped instrumentation (step 1 → step 4) and verification failed. Now re-entering at step 1: instrumentation is MANDATORY this time.
step.action: Go to step 2 (Add Instrumentation) — do NOT skip to step 4 again.
step.escape_clause: If instrumentation output is genuinely unavailable in the user's environment (e.g., no console access, no log file capability, or environment restrictions — see error_handling: instrumentation_output_unavailable): proceed with static analysis, process-of-elimination, and assertion-based narrowing. State clearly in the completion report: "Instrumentation was mandated by retry rule but output was unavailable in this environment. Investigation proceeded via [methods]." Do NOT add [DEBUG] statements whose output can never be read.

step.title: Apply Fix
step.description:
Apply the fix to the identified root cause.
CRITICAL: Never modify E2E tests. The existing E2E test is the authority — fix the source code, not the E2E test. E2E tests validate real user behavior; changing them to match broken code defeats their purpose.
For unit and integration tests that depended on buggy behavior: updating test expectations to match correct behavior IS appropriate — the test was wrong, not the fix. The "never modify tests" rule applies specifically to E2E tests.

constraints.constraint: Do not modify E2E test files — fix the source code instead
Unit/integration tests with wrong expectations due to buggy behavior SHOULD be updated
Prefer minimal, targeted changes over broad refactors
Follow existing code patterns and conventions

step.title: Run Existing Tests
step.description:
Run the existing automated test suite to verify the fix doesn't cause regressions.
Test scope: - Unit tests: ALWAYS - Integration tests: ALWAYS - E2E tests: ONLY if the original failure was an E2E test. E2E tests are slow — running them at this stage AND at step 8 is wasteful. Prefer running E2E only at step 8 (the final gate).

branch.action: Proceed to step 6
Fix ALL failures — whether caused by your fix or pre-existing. Debug leaves things greener.
branch.scope_exception: Exception: a pre-existing failure may be too large in scope for Debug to fix (requires changes to 5+ files, database schema changes, API contract changes, or architectural restructuring). In this case: document the failure, its root cause if identified, and why it's out of scope. Mark as "documented out-of-scope failure." Continue to step 6. Documented out-of-scope failures are excluded from step 8's all-green requirement. If in doubt whether something is too large: fix it.
branch.e2e_test_exception: Exception: if an E2E test failure is determined to be a bug in the E2E test itself (wrong selector, outdated expectation, timing bug in the test — not the application), do NOT fix the application code to match a buggy test. Follow the error_handling: e2e_fix_requires_modifying_e2e_test scenario — document the test bug, do not modify the E2E test, and report it.
branch.action: Diagnose each fixable failure → fix → re-run from step 5
branch.circuit_breaker: Each test tracks its own failure counter. Step 5 and step 8 share the same counter for the same test — a failure at step 5 counts toward the same test's 3-attempt limit. After 3 genuinely different approaches on the same test: stop. Report what was found, what was tried, and the current state. Do not loop indefinitely.

branch.description:
Tests passing = verified. No user interaction.
For the fix to be verifiable autonomously, there must be an automated test that reproduces the issue (or the existing test suite itself demonstrates the fix). If no automated test reproduces the issue (e.g., a smoke test failure spawned in hands-off mode without a corresponding automated test): this scenario is not supported in hands-off mode. Report: "Cannot verify autonomously — no automated test reproduces the issue. Verification requires human interaction."
branch.action: Proceed to step 7

branch.description: Ask the user to confirm the fix works.
branch.action: Use ask_followup_question: "Fix applied to [files]. Please verify by [exact reproduction steps]. Does it work correctly now?"
Proceed to step 7
Re-enter at step 1 to re-analyze.

branch.condition: Instrumentation was NOT attempted in the previous cycle
branch.action: Step 3 mandates instrumentation this time. Proceed to step 2 (do not re-attempt a code-analysis-only fix).
Analyze what the logs revealed vs. what was missed. Refine understanding. Try a fundamentally different fix approach → go to step 4 with the refined understanding.

step.title: Write Regression Test
step.description:
Write a test that reproduces the original failure. The fix is already applied at this point, so verify the test PASSES. By code inspection, confirm the test targets the exact code path and scenario that was broken — it would fail if the bug were re-introduced.
The regression test should: - Pass with the fix applied (verifiable now) - Target the specific scenario, inputs, and code path of the original failure - Be placed in the appropriate test directory (unit/integration; not E2E unless spawned for E2E)
When fixing an E2E failure: write a unit or integration-level regression test that captures the root cause at a lower, faster level. Do not modify the E2E test itself.

step.title: Run All Tests
step.description:
Final verification: run the complete test suite including the new regression test.
Test scope: - Unit tests: ALWAYS - Integration tests: ALWAYS - Regression test: ALWAYS - E2E tests: ONLY if the original failure was an E2E test (run here once, not at step 5)
Pre-existing failures that were documented as out-of-scope at step 5 are excluded from the all-green requirement. Report them but do not loop on them.

branch.condition: All tests pass (excluding documented out-of-scope failures)
branch.action: Report success: root cause, fix applied, regression test written, all fixable tests green. Done.

branch.condition: The regression test itself is wrong (not the fix)
branch.action: Fix the regression test and re-run step 8 only. Do not restart from step 5.
Diagnose the issue → go to step 4 (apply new fix) → proceed through steps 5-8.

branch.circuit_breaker: Each test tracks its own counter, shared with step 5. After 3 genuinely different approaches on the same test: report findings and escalate.

phase.name: multi_issue_workflow
phase.description: For investigating and fixing multiple bugs or test failures in one spawn.

step.title: Triage All Issues
step.description: Analyze all issues against the codebase. Determine the optimal fix order by: - Dependencies: issue A's fix is a prerequisite for issue B - Risk: simplest/smallest first to build momentum and reduce variables - Impact: most critical first if no dependency constraints

branch.action: Present the ordered plan to the user: "I'll fix in this order: [1 → 2 → ... → N]. Proceed?"
If user wants a different order: adjust and re-confirm
Proceed with the determined order autonomously

step.title: Fix Each Issue One at a Time
step.description:
For each issue in order, follow the single-issue workflow (steps 1-6 only): 1. Analyze evidence 2. Instrument if needed (with the 3-round limit) 3. Mandatory instrumentation on retry (if applicable, with the unavailable-output escape) 4. Apply fix 5. Run existing tests (with the out-of-scope exception and E2E-test-bug exception) 6. Verify (autonomous in hands-off; ask user in interactive)
Do NOT write regression tests yet — they will be batched after all fixes are verified.

constraint.rule: After fixing issue N, verify it before moving to issue N+1
If fixing issue N+1 breaks issue N: re-fix issue N before continuing

step.circuit_breaker_handling: If any single issue hits the circuit breaker during its sub-flow: - Preserve all previously-verified fixes (do not revert them) - Write regression tests for all verified fixes immediately - Run the full test suite for the verified fixes to confirm they're green - Report partial success: "[N] issues fixed with regression tests. [1] issue unresolved: [description]. Circuit breaker triggered after 3 approaches: [summary]." - Do NOT lose verified work. Do NOT continue trying the unresolved issue.

step.title: Combined Test Run (All Fixes)
step.description:
After all issues are fixed individually and verified per-issue, run the full unit + integration test suite once with ALL fixes applied together. This catches cross-fix interactions before final verification and before regression tests are written.
If any test fails: investigate whether it's a cross-fix interaction. Re-fix the affected issue(s) and re-run this combined suite.

branch.action: Ask user: "All [N] issues fixed. Please verify everything works correctly end-to-end."
Identify which issue → re-enter single-issue workflow for that issue.
After re-fixing: return to step 3 (final verification) — do NOT re-enter the per-issue loop at step 2.
Combined test suite from step 2b passing + per-issue verifications from step 2 = verified. Proceed to step 4.

step.title: Batch Regression Tests
step.description: Write regression tests for ALL fixes together. Run each regression test individually to confirm it passes.

step.title: Run Full Test Suite
step.description:
Run the complete test suite: unit + integration + all new regression tests (+ E2E if any original failure was an E2E test).
Pre-existing failures documented as out-of-scope are excluded from the all-green requirement.

branch.condition: All tests pass (excluding documented out-of-scope failures)
branch.action: Report success: N issues fixed, N regression tests written, all fixable tests green. Done.
Diagnose → fix → re-run
branch.circuit_breaker: Each test tracks its own counter. After 3 genuinely different approaches on the same test: report findings and escalate.

circuit_breaker_criteria.description: What counts as a "genuinely different" approach vs. a variation:

genuinely_different_examples.example: Fix the data layer vs. fix the business logic vs. fix the presentation layer
Change the algorithm vs. add input validation vs. fix the data source
Fix a race condition with a lock vs. restructure to avoid shared state vs. use atomic operations

not_genuinely_different_examples.example: Adjusting a timeout from 100ms to 200ms to 300ms
Changing a constant value incrementally until tests pass

completion_criteria.criterion: Fix applied and verified (by tests or user)
All existing fixable tests pass (unit + integration; E2E if applicable)
Documented out-of-scope failures are reported but do not block completion
No instrumentation or debug statements left in production code
